basic_details.job_title: Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 1265 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Society for Centralized Recruitment of Staff in Subordinate Courts (S.S.S.C.) under High Court of Punjab and Haryana, Chandigarh
basic_details.department: Subordinate Courts of Haryana (on behalf of District and Sessions Judges, Haryana)
basic_details.total_vacancies: 1265 (1015 existing posts as on 31.12.2025 + 250 anticipated posts upto 30.06.2026)
basic_details.job_type: Regular / Permanent (candidates must be willing to serve anywhere in Haryana State)
basic_details.short_information: SSSC Haryana is recruiting 1265 Clerk posts for Subordinate Courts of Haryana with Bachelor's Degree qualification, last date 23 June 2026.

important_dates.qualification cut-off date: 23-06-2026 (closing date for submission of online applications)
important_dates.demo / mock test link availability: 15 days prior to the date of examination (on www.sssc.gov.in)
important_dates.exam_date: To be notified on www.sssc.gov.in (can be conducted anywhere in Northern India)
important_dates.computer proficiency test (cpt) date: To be notified after Written Examination result
important_dates.document verification / interaction date: To be notified after CPT result

application_fee.details: Application fee is to be paid online only and is non-refundable. The registration process is complete only when fee is paid online. Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstance. Applicants opting "Others" in Gender column shall be charged fee as per their category in the above table.

age_limit.details: Age limit for the post of Clerks is calculated as on 01-01-2026. The upper age limit of any applicant (except Ex-servicemen) shall not exceed 52 years even after availing all applicable age relaxations.

qualification.education_qualification: Bachelor of Arts (BA) or Bachelor of Science (BSc) or equivalent from a recognized university with proficiency in computers (Word Processing and Spread Sheets).
qualification.domicile: As per Haryana Subordinate Courts Establishment (Recruitment and General Conditions of Service) Rules, 1997. Reserved category benefits only for bonafide Haryana residents.
qualification.details: Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions by the closing date of online application i.e. 23-06-2026. Only bonafide residents of Haryana State are eligible for reserved category benefits. Candidates of reserved categories of other states must apply under General category. Only one application per category is permitted - if more than one application is submitted, only the latest will be considered.

vacancy_details.Category: Scheduled Castes of Haryana - Deprived SC (DSC)

vacancy_details.Category: OA, BA, OL, BL, OAL, LC, Dw, AAV

Specific Learning Disability, Mental Illness, and Multiple Disabilities involving categories (a) to (d) above

category_wise_vacancy.Description: Specific Learning Disability, Mental Illness, and Multiple Disabilities involving categories (a) to (d) above

salary_details.pay_scale: As per latest rules and instructions issued by the Government of Haryana adopted for Subordinate Courts of Haryana

selection_process: Sr. No. Subject Type No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
1 General Knowledge Objective (MCQ) 50 questions (1 mark each) 50 2 Hours
2 English Composition Objective (MCQ) 50 questions (1 mark each) 20
Subjective - Essay (150 words) -- 10 Within overall 2 hours
Subjective - Letter -- 05
Subjective - Precis -- 05
Subjective - Translation (Hindi to English) -- 10
Total 100
Selection for the post of Clerk will be based on (i) Online Written Examination - Computer Based Test (CBT)
followed by (ii) Computer Proficiency Test (CPT) of qualifying nature
and (iii) checking of original testimonials/interaction. Final merit list will be prepared strictly on the basis of marks obtained in the Online Written Examination (CBT). In case of equal marks
the elder candidate in age will be ranked higher; if date of birth is also same
candidates will be placed alphabetically (A-Z). No TA/DA will be paid for appearing in examination/interaction.
Stage 1 - Online Written Examination (Computer Based Test - Objective + Subjective)

Qualifying marks: 33% in each subject separately; minimum 40% in aggregate of both subjects. There will be negative marking for wrong answers in MCQ - 1/4th mark will be deducted for each wrong answer. Questions not attempted receive no credit or discredit. English Composition (Subjective) will be evaluated only for candidates who score 33% or more in General Knowledge. Subjective part of English Composition must be typed on computer within the stipulated time. If the examination is held in multiple shifts
normalization of scores of the objective portion shall be carried out using the Standard Deviation Method. Marks for ranking = normalized marks (objective) + actual marks (subjective).
Stage 2 - Computer Proficiency Test (CPT) - Qualifying Nature
Marks of CPT will NOT be counted in determining final merit - it is purely qualifying. CPT has two parts. Part-I: Spread Sheet Test - 10 marks

10 minutes duration; qualifying marks: 40% (4 or more marks). Part-II: Computer Typing Test in English - minimum 30 W.P.M. (Typing Speed = (Words Typed - Mistakes) ÷ 10). Part-II will be checked only for candidates who score 4 or more marks in Part-I. PwBD candidates with Both Arms Affected (BA) or those with physical limitation to type at the requisite speed (certified by CMO/Civil Surgeon/Medical Superintendent of a Government healthcare institution) may apply for exemption from CPT within 15 days from declaration of CBT result.
Stage 3 - Checking of Original Testimonials / Interaction
Candidates who qualify both CBT and CPT will be called for checking of original documents/interaction as per numbers decided by the High Court
category-wise. Final Select List is prepared strictly on the basis of CBT merit.
Answer Key and Objections
After CBT

a provisional Answer Key will be published on www.sssc.gov.in. Candidates may raise objections at a nominal non-refundable fee of Rs. 25/- per objection. Cross-objections against proposed changes in the provisional answer key are also permitted. Objections/cross-objections must be supported by reasons and documentary proof; those received after stipulated time or through any medium other than the prescribed link will not be considered.
how_to_apply: Applications must be submitted in online mode only under the head "Clerk Subordinate Courts of Haryana" on the official website www.sssc.gov.in. No other means or mode of submission will be accepted. Applicants must first complete a Registration Form followed by the Application Form, which is linked to the fee payment gateway. The online application form is submitted only after successful payment of fee. Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date and not wait till 23-06-2026 at 04:00 PM. Incomplete applications will be rejected outright without any communication.
Information/Documents to be kept ready before filling Online Application Form:
Personal details (name, date of birth, category, domicile details etc.)
Valid and active personal email ID (to be kept active throughout the recruitment process)
Valid and active mobile number (to be kept active throughout the recruitment process)
Online payment facility - Internet Banking / Debit Card / Credit Card / UPI
Scanned recent passport-size photograph (not more than one month old, white background)
Real-time photograph (to be captured via inbuilt/web camera at the time of filling the form - computer/device with working camera is required)
Valid reservation certificate (OBC/SC/EWS/ESM/PwBD/ESP as applicable) - must be latest and valid as on 23-06-2026

Printout/hardcopy of the Online Application Form should be retained by the applicant and produced as and when required. Re-checking of answer sheets (not re-evaluation) can be requested within 30 days of declaration of final result at a fee of Rs. 500/- per answer sheet via Indian Postal Order payable to S.S.S.C.

For Technical queries: Helpline No. +91 99866-38762 (Working days, 09:30 AM to 05:00 PM). For other queries about terms and conditions: Phone No. 0172-2722012 and 9115898394 (Working days, 09:30 AM to 05:00 PM).
important_instructions: Applications must be submitted online only at www.sssc.gov.in - no other mode of submission will be accepted. All particulars including category and sub-category once filled in the Online Application Form are sacrosanct - no change will be permitted at any stage. Only one application per category is permitted. If more than one application is submitted, only the latest will be considered and fee for earlier applications will be forfeited. The real-time photograph, scanned photograph, and photograph pasted on E-Admit Card must all match - mismatch results in denial of entry at the examination centre. E-Admit Cards will be uploaded on www.sssc.gov.in only - Admit Cards will NOT be sent by post. Mere issuance of provisional E-Admit Card does not confirm eligibility. No mobile phones, electronic devices, watches, jewellery items, or baggage material are allowed inside the examination centre. Examination can be conducted anywhere in Northern India on any suitable date(s). No request for change of test centre, city, date, or shift will be entertained. No TA/DA will be paid to applicants for appearing in examination or checking of original testimonials/interaction. All candidates must give an undertaking in the Online Application Form that they will work anywhere in Haryana State where they are posted. Benefit of reservation under any category will be given only to bonafide residents of Haryana State with certificates issued by the Competent Authority of Government of Haryana. Certificates of reserved categories of other states are not valid. Re-evaluation of answer sheets is not permitted. Only re-checking (totalling error check) is allowed within 30 days of final result at Rs. 500/- per answer sheet. Canvassing in any form will entail cancellation of candidature. Any attempt to obtain support by unfair means will render the applicant liable for disqualification and legal action. The High Court reserves the right to alter/modify or change any of the terms and conditions including selection criteria. The decision of the High Court on all matters is final and binding. In case of any discrepancy between the advertisement published in newspapers and the official website content, the content on www.sssc.gov.in shall prevail. Success in the examination confers no right of appointment unless the Competent Authority is satisfied that the candidate is suitable in all respects for the service/post.

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The High Court of Punjab and Haryana has announced a total of 1265 Clerk vacancies under the Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026. This includes 1015 existing posts and 250 anticipated vacancies up to June 2026. The number of vacancies may change depending on administrative requirements at the time of final selection

faq.question: Q2. What is the eligibility criteria for Haryana SSSC Clerk 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a Bachelor’s degree (BA, BSc, or equivalent) from a recognized university along with basic computer proficiency in Word Processing and Spreadsheets. Additionally, applicants must have studied Hindi or Sanskrit at Matric level or higher. All qualifications must be completed before 23 June 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 42 years for General category candidates as on 01 January 2026. Reserved categories like SC, BC, PwBD, and others are eligible for age relaxation as per Haryana government rules. However, the upper age limit cannot exceed 52 years after all relaxations.

faq.question: Q4. What is the last date to apply for Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Vacancy 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process will start on 26 May 2026 at 4:00 PM and will remain open until 23 June 2026 at 4:00 PM. Candidates are advised to complete their application well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical issues. Late submissions will not be accepted under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of three stages: Computer Based Test (CBT), Computer Proficiency Test (CPT), and document verification. The final merit list is prepared based on marks obtained in the CBT only. The CPT is qualifying in nature and must be cleared to proceed further.

faq.question: Q6. What is the exam pattern of Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk CBT 2026?
faq.answer: The CBT includes General Knowledge and English Composition sections with both objective and subjective questions. The exam carries 100 marks with a duration of 2 hours, and there is negative marking of 1/4 for incorrect answers. Candidates must score at least 33% in each section and 40% overall to qualify.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for Haryana Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, the application fee varies based on category and gender. General category males need to pay Rs. 1200, while females pay Rs. 875. Haryana reserved categories pay Rs. 710, and PwBD candidates are exempted from paying any fee.

faq.question: Q8. Who can apply for Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk posts under reserved categories?
faq.answer: Only bonafide residents of Haryana are eligible to claim reservation benefits under SC, BC, EWS, ESM, and other categories. Candidates from other states must apply under the General category even if they belong to a reserved category. Valid and updated certificates are mandatory at the time of application.

faq.question: Q9. What is the salary of Haryana High Court Clerk 2026?
faq.answer: The pay scale for Clerk posts will be as per the latest rules and instructions issued by the Government of Haryana. Along with basic pay, selected candidates will also receive allowances and benefits applicable to state government employees. The job is permanent and offers stable career growth.

faq.question: Q10. How to apply online for Punjab and Haryana High Court Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply online through the official website sssc.gov.in by completing registration, filling the application form, uploading documents, and paying the fee. It is important to keep scanned documents, a valid email ID, and a working mobile number ready before applying. The application is considered complete only after successful fee payment.
